title: What an American thinks about Panama

transcript.text: What an American thinks about Panama.
I was there like 10 years ago, in 2000.
Panama is really cosmopolitan.
I saw people from all over, India, China, Africa,
And they were dressed in their traditional garb.
All these people from all these races from all over the world
Dressed in their traditional dress,
Like from 100, 200 years ago dress.
You know, like the Africans in their African dress.
Indians in their Indian dress
And the little things painted in their head.
Really interesting.
Yeah, and the city, Panama City, is very, you know,
First world-ish, big buildings.
Nicely kept streets.
Streets, super malls, all that stuff.
I did see a lot of, like, black people
Trying to sell jewelry on the street, though.
And they did have a ghetto section next to where I was.
My hotel was right there next to the central plaza.
And it was like the old town, but it was still cheap.
But right next to that was this ghetto area.
Just big buildings with lots of people living in them.
And I rode to the top of a town and looked down on the city.
And it's not that far from the docks.
Or not the docks.
What are those things called?
That's where the Panama Canal is.
And they have those things that make the boats lower.
You can actually see it from going and watch it.
Yeah, and they have to, like, lower the boat a little bit.
Because one side of the ocean there is higher than the other.
And if they don't have those docks,
You'd have, like, a river going through.
And those boats would be doing bumper boats all the way down.
So they have to control it, those docks.
Those things.
And that was interesting to watch.
Yeah, they speak really good English.
And they use the dollar.
And, yeah.

That's all I remember about Panama.
